methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: ERC-20 tokens held by a Well pool (referenced via Well.tokens(); actual custody lives in Well contract, not shown here)
LP token supply/value represented by Stable2.calcLpTokenSupply invariant math (used to mint/burn Well LP tokens in Well, not shown here)
Upgradeable Well implementation addresses (WellUpgradeable upgrade target); while not a financial asset itself, it gates control over pool behavior and thus indirectly controls all pooled funds
Aquifer-bored minimal proxy Well instances (EIP-1167 clones) that may hold reserves and LP state
state_assets: Lookup table data returned by Stable2LUT1.getRatiosFromPriceLiquidity/getRatiosFromPriceSwap (hardcoded estimation bands)
Stable2 immutable configuration: lookupTable (address), a (amplification parameter)
Stable2 algorithmic outputs: calcRate, calcReserve, calcReserveAtRatioSwap, calcReserveAtRatioLiquidity (pricing/ratio/reserve computation used by Well)
WellUpgradeable UUPS state (ERC1967 implementation slot) used for upgrades
WellUpgradeable initialization state: Initializable versioning (_getInitializedVersion) and reinitializer(2) guard for init()
WellUpgradeable ownership state (OwnableUpgradeable): owner(), pending ownership (if OZ variant), etc. (storage in inherited contracts)
Well core state inherited by WellUpgradeable (not provided): token list, well function + pumps configuration, wellData/immutableData pointers, reserves/accounting, LP token balances, etc.
privilege_assets: WellUpgradeable upgrade authority (UUPS upgradeTo/upgradeToAndCall; ultimately controlled by whatever access control is implemented—NOTE: _authorizeUpgrade as written does not check owner/role)
WellUpgradeable owner role (OwnableUpgradeable) and any owner-only capabilities inherited from Well (not shown)
Initializer/reinitializer authority on WellUpgradeable: init() (reinitializer(2)) and initNoWellToken() (initializer)
Aquifer governance/control over what is a valid Well implementation via IAquifer.wellImplementation(mapping)
reputation_assets: Pool pricing integrity derived from Stable2 math + LUT correctness (errors/manipulation can cause mispricing, loss, or protocol trust damage)
Upgrade trust in WellUpgradeable implementation correctness and authorization (upgrade bypass/bricking damages protocol credibility)
Aquifer factory verification trust assumptions (correct mapping of proxies to implementations)

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: Stable2.sol inherits ProportionalLPToken2 and implements IBeanstalkWellFunction (and via interface compatibility IMultiFlowPumpWellFunction for calcRate/calcReserveAtRatio*). WellUpgradeable.sol inherits Well (core pool/LP token), UUPSUpgradeable, and OwnableUpgradeable. WellDeployer is abstract (no inheritance shown) and provides internal deployment helpers. Stable2LUT1 implements ILookupTable. Logger is a library.
external_dependencies: Forge std: IERC20 interface (forge-std/interfaces/IERC20.sol) used by Stable2; console/console2 for Logger

Protocol-specific: Well, Aquifer, IAquifer, ILookupTable, IBeanstalkWellFunction, IMultiFlowPumpWellFunction, LibWellConstructor, LibWellUpgradeableConstructor, LibContractInfo (some imported but not used in snippet)
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: WellUpgradeable uses UUPS (ERC1967 implementation slot) but is designed to be deployed behind EIP-1167 minimal proxies bored by an Aquifer. upgradeTo/upgradeToAndCall are overridden to bypass OZ's onlyProxy-style restrictions and to add Aquifer-based validation. No Diamond pattern detected in provided files.
system_architecture.access_control_structure: WellUpgradeable includes OwnableUpgradeable initialization, but its upgrade authorization _authorizeUpgrade() does NOT check owner/onlyOwner; instead it checks (1) call is via delegatecall, (2) current implementation is considered 'active' by Aquifer mapping, (3) new implementation is recognized by Aquifer mapping and is proxiable. Stable2/Stable2LUT1 are pure/view math utilities with no access control. WellDeployer functions are internal-only. Logger is a library function callable by any contract that links it.

external_calls: calcReserve(scaledReserves, i, lpTokenSupply, abi.encode(18,18)) (called repeatedly in loop)
_calcRate(scaledReserves, i, j, lpTokenSupply) (called repeatedly in loop)
calcRate(scaledReserves, i, j, abi.encode(18,18)) (called repeatedly in loop)
IAquifer via Well.aquifer() indirectly not called here
delegatecall into newImplementation with data (performed by OZ internals)

entry_points.contract: WellUpgradeable.sol (inherited but not fully visible in snippet)
WellUpgradeable.sol (inherited but not fully visible in snippet)

upgradeability_surface.notes: UUPS with custom minimal-proxy (EIP-1167) compatibility checks via Aquifer mapping. _authorizeUpgrade() lacks any explicit onlyOwner/role check; relies solely on Aquifer/implementation validation. Multiple init paths exist (init reinitializer(2) and initNoWellToken initializer), creating mis-initialization/bricking risk.
Non-upgradeable pure/view pricing math with immutable LUT address and A parameter.
Abstract helper with internal deployment functions only; not directly callable externally.
Dev logging library using forge-std console; not intended for production deployments.
